title: The Border
openLocation: Southeastern Eversong Woods, on Farstrider patrol near the Amani frontier.
transport: Thalassian horses for Sylvanas, Lor'themar, and the mounted patrol.
immediateObjective: Finish the troll sweep, dodge the Convocation budget session, and stay outdoors one more day.
constraints: Jaina does not speak on-page until the chapter-ending hook.

povKnowsAtOpen: Routine Amani troll pressure on the southeastern frontier.
The ward network is stable as far as the formal reports say.
The Farstriders maintain dossiers on powerful human mages near the border.
Jaina Proudmoore appears in those reports as a human mage worth watching.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: That a human mage has crossed the ward boundary.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No trust between Sylvanas and Jaina.
No proof yet that this is anything more than a human trespass.
No dialogue from Jaina before the final line.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Jaina is in Farstrider custody and must make her case directly to Sylvanas.
Sylvanas knows the prisoner is Jaina Proudmoore, not a random trespasser.
The border breach has turned from patrol annoyance into a serious interrogation.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: Anything south of the wards stays somebody else's trouble until it touches Sylvanas's stones, roads, or riders.
pov.surfaceObjective: Finish the troll work, stay on horseback, and find one more excuse to miss the Convocation.
pov.pressureSource: Cyndia's runner finds her with word that a human mage crossed the wards and will not stop talking about something wrong in the leylines.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Sylvanas hears human mage and thinks politics, spying, paperwork. If the trouble started in Lordaeron, she expects Lordaeron to keep it there.
pov.chapterTest: The first warning that matters comes from the exact kind of outsider she is built to distrust, and it lands inside her own border instead of safely beyond it.
pov.forcedChoice: Leave the prisoner to protocol and let other people sort the mess out, or ride south and put her own eyes on it.
pov.endStateShift: By chapter close this is no longer a spoiled patrol day. It is Sylvanas's problem, and the mage is standing in her holding space.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 2 opens with Sylvanas angry, alert.

throughPov.visibleFunction: The captured human mage who ruins an easy day before she even opens her mouth.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: A Kirin Tor intrusion, a spy, or some polished court sorceress who expects the border to bend for her.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Before Jaina even speaks, the worn boots, ink stains, frost-bitten knuckles, and raw-edged posture say fieldwork, exhaustion, and frustration instead of court polish.
throughPov.interactionRule: Inside Sylvanas's head, Jaina stays the mage, the human, or the prisoner. No warmth. No name-softening.
throughPov.doNotRevealYet: The full contamination case, her isolation from the Kirin Tor, or her desperation.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: No partnership. One woman in custody, one woman deciding how hard the door stays shut.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: Jaina stops being paperwork trouble and becomes the one person Sylvanas has to hear before she can decide what kind of threat this is.
team.namingAttitudeRule: Sylvanas does not give Jaina the dignity of her name in her internal framing yet.
team.doNotOverstateRule: No thaw, no rapport, no early respect.

entries.rule: Present in Scenes 1-2. Exits north in Scene 2 to brief the Convocation.
Present in Scenes 1-2. Leaves north with Lor'themar once the report lands.
Enters in Scene 3 as border-post officer. Remains active into Chapter 2.
Scene 2 only. Delivers the report, then returns south with Sylvanas.
Seen in Scene 3. She does not speak on-page until the chapter-ending hook.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 2 opens at Cyndia's post on the interrogation beat. Lor'themar and the mounted patrol are already northbound; Sylvanas, Jaina, and Cyndia inherit the scene.

scenes.title: The Hunt
scenes.sceneFunction: Show Sylvanas where she is happiest, and the easy rhythm she only has with Lor'themar.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Ordinary frontier work; nothing stranger than trolls.
scenes.arcPressure: Sylvanas's field ease is about to be interrupted by exactly the kind of outsider problem she prefers to leave south of the wards.

enrichment.detail: Eversong is canonically held in a perpetual-spring state and defended by runestones that blunt hostile magic, so the patrol can read as controlled golden woodland rather than wild frontier chaos.
enrichment.source: Warcraft Wiki: Eversong Woods; local ref: stories/the-plague-road/locations/silvermoon-and-quelthalas.md

enrichment.detail: Farstrider mounts read best here as lightly tacked Thalassian field horses — endurance-bred, quiet under the ward hum, expensive but visibly field-used.

beats.goal: Finish the sweep by killing the last troll raider.
beats.conflict: The final Amani axethrower breaks from cover with an attack already committed.
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas is at ease only on a live patrol.
She sees footing, range, and openings before anyone else does.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Any larger threat than routine troll violence.
beats.transitionIntent: release-then-drift: the clean kill opens a pocket of banter and reminds the reader how badly Sylvanas wants to stay out here.

beats.dilemma: Ride back for the Convocation budget session or steal one more stretch of road before duty catches up.
beats.decision: Stretch the patrol south under the excuse of checking the ward-stones.
newOnPageInformation: Lor'themar knows exactly when Sylvanas is lying to herself.
The Convocation is a standing pressure on Sylvanas's time and temperament.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the ward-stones actually need checking.
beats.transitionIntent: ease-extension: let the road stay easy for another minute.

beats.conflict: Lor'themar keeps pressing the political reality Sylvanas wants to ignore.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets the ride she wants, but the Convocation remains waiting at the edge of the day.

newOnPageInformation: Lor'themar is the one who absorbs court irritation so Sylvanas can stay where she likes best.
This is Sylvanas's emotional baseline before the story disrupts it.
Cyndia would know exactly which southbound road to search if Sylvanas stole one more patrol before a Convocation day.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Any immediate threat south of them.
sensoryAnchors: shared pace on the forest road
beats.transitionIntent: hard-interruption: ease is broken by an urgent mounted approach from the south.

scenes.title: The Messenger
scenes.sceneFunction: Bring in the bad report and split Sylvanas from Lor'themar.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Still just a border day, as far as anyone knows.
scenes.arcPressure: The problem Sylvanas wanted to leave south of the wards has already reached her road.

enrichment.detail: The southern border should feel like a real threshold: the golden Eversong ambience starts thinning, the ward-stones carry a bass vibration felt in the teeth, and the pass wind never quite stops.
enrichment.source: Local ref: stories/the-plague-road/locations/silvermoon-and-quelthalas.md; Warcraft lore summaries on Eversong/Quel'Thalas defenses

beats.conflict: A Farstrider runner comes in hard from Cyndia's post with word that a human mage crossed the first ward ring before dawn and was caught studying the ward-stones.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas loses the rest of her ordinary day, and the trouble immediately smells like Kirin Tor business and politics.

newOnPageInformation: There is a detained human mage inside the ward network.
She has no presented credentials.
She is claiming something is wrong in the leylines.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether she acts for the Kirin Tor, for herself, or for someone else.
beats.transitionIntent: command-pressure: someone has to handle the politics, and someone has to ride south.
beats.dialogueGuidance: Lor'themar should underline, dryly, that Cyndia predicted Sylvanas would dodge the Convocation and sent the runner up the south patrol road on purpose.

beats.dilemma: Someone must contain the political fallout while someone with real authority evaluates the prisoner.
beats.decision: Lor'themar rides north to the Convocation; Sylvanas rides south to the Pass.
newOnPageInformation: Lor'themar goes where councils talk; Sylvanas goes where trouble is still alive.
Both understand the stakes of an unauthorized human mage inside the ward system.
The patrol goes north with Lor'themar while the runner wheels back south with Sylvanas.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether this is a diplomatic mess, a spy problem, or something worse.
sensoryAnchors: north road versus hard southern track
sudden gap after Lor'themar peels away
beats.transitionIntent: isolation-pressure: Sylvanas rides south without the one person who usually takes the edge off.

beats.goal: Reach the border post fast enough to control the situation personally.
beats.conflict: On the ride down, Sylvanas keeps turning over the possibilities — rogue mage, Kirin Tor spying, someone tampering with the wards — and none of them fit cleanly.

outcome.summary: She arrives angrier and more certain it matters, but the talk of wrong leylines still does not fit any box she trusts.

newOnPageInformation: The border transition is both physical and mental for Sylvanas.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the mage is in over her head or deliberately hostile.
That the prisoner is Jaina Proudmoore.
beats.transitionIntent: arrival-pressure: the road carries Sylvanas straight into Cyndia's account.

scenes.title: The Prisoner
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn a border arrest into a real problem and end on the hook.
scenes.arcPressure: Seeing Jaina up close keeps this from staying a report on paper. Now it is a woman, a name, and a problem inside Sylvanas's own border post.

enrichment.detail: Canon Jaina at this point is Antonidas's apprentice and already treated as an exceptional young mage; age twenty-three to twenty-four is the right band for her first appearance here.
enrichment.source: Warcraft Wiki: Jaina Proudmoore; Wowhead lore guide for Jaina Proudmoore

enrichment.detail: Canon Sylvanas is already the Ranger-General of Silvermoon and commander of the Farstriders, so her read of Jaina as a sovereign-security breach rather than a mere trespass is fully grounded.
enrichment.source: Warcraft Wiki: Sylvanas Windrunner; Wowpedia: Ranger-General

beats.goal: Establish exactly what happened at the border.
beats.conflict: Cyndia confirms the mage crossed before dawn, reached the second ring, and argued for continued analysis instead of escape.

outcome.summary: Cyndia gives Sylvanas clean facts, but the facts make the prisoner stranger, not clearer.

newOnPageInformation: The mage was not sneaking carefully; she was intent on her work.
The ward-stones reacted like something had gone wrong in them, not like someone blasted a path through.
Cyndia confiscated staff and materials pending senior authority.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the mage is arrogant, panicked, or both.
Whether the leyline warning is genuine.
beats.transitionIntent: containment-pressure: the report sends Sylvanas straight to the holding space.

beats.goal: Assess the prisoner and confirm identity.
beats.conflict: What Sylvanas expected does not match what she sees: practical travel gear, worn boots, calloused hands, frost-burned knuckles, and the posture of someone furious at not being heard.

outcome.summary: The woman in the holding space is not what Sylvanas expected, and Cyndia names her at the same moment: Jaina Proudmoore, Antonidas's apprentice, inside the ward grid.

newOnPageInformation: The prisoner is Jaina Proudmoore.
She does not read as a pampered court mage.
This is no longer a minor trespass case.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Why Jaina crossed the border alone.
Whether Antonidas knows where she is.
Whether the contamination claim is real.
beats.transitionIntent: escalation-hook: identity turns detention into interrogation.

beats.dilemma: Releasing Jaina is impossible, but holding her without hearing her out feels just as dangerous.
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas now treats Jaina as a real security problem.
The conversation itself becomes the chapter hook.
sensoryAnchors: absolute stillness before the first exchange
beats.transitionIntent: hard-hook: Sylvanas steps into interrogation and the chapter cuts on her opening demand.

departuresLocked: Lor'themar is northbound toward Silvermoon and will not appear physically in Chapter 2.
The mounted patrol goes north with Lor'themar and is gone before Sylvanas reaches the outpost.
The runner returns to border-post duty after bringing Sylvanas in and does not remain active in Chapter 2.
factsLockedForChapter2: Jaina has been detained for hours before Sylvanas arrives.
Sylvanas knows Jaina's identity before the conversation starts.
Jaina has not yet explained the contamination on-page.
The line that opens the interrogation belongs to the chapter boundary and must not be replayed from outside Jaina's POV in Chapter 2.
factsNotYetEarned: Any assumption that the danger has already become something everyone can see and name.

warnings: Source outline omitted Scene 1 arcPressure; normalized from the chapter's stated conflict.
Beats 1-3 all shift upward (tension -> satisfaction -> playful defiance -> ease). Keep the opener brisk so the runner's arrival feels like a clean interruption rather than drift.

craftResearch.findings: Open inside a live moment, avoid exposition-first starts, and raise a story question immediately. This plan does that by opening on a kill shot and withholding the real border threat until the runner arrives.
craftResearch.source: Konn Lavery; What We Writing; Writer's Digest

craftResearch.findings: Introduce the world through action and consequence rather than explanation. The plan uses ward-hum, Thalassian horses, and border protocol as lived texture instead of a lore dump.
craftResearch.source: Writer's Digest; Fabled Planet; What We Writing

craftResearch.findings: Action chapters still need short reaction/decision beats after pressure spikes. Beats 2, 5, and 9 already provide the required emotional and decision breathing room.
craftResearch.source: Dabble scene-and-sequel guidance; BubbleCow; Jack Bickham references

craftResearch.findings: Chapters should rise, briefly release, then climb into a sharper turn instead of staying flat or relentless. This chapter dips into ease before the messenger interruption, then ratchets upward scene by scene.

A strong chapter ending leaves a consequential question unresolved rather than fully resolving the scene. Ending on Sylvanas committing to interrogate Jaina creates a clean interrogative hook into chapter 2.
craftResearch.source: Writer's Digest Online; Helping Writers Become Authors; RiverEditor

structure: Beat 1 opens in committed action, immediately establishing competence and avoiding a static setup.
Beats 1-3 intentionally descend from combat into ease; because the span is only three beats, it reads as baseline establishment rather than an opening sag.
Beat 4 lands the real chapter turn at the chapter midpoint, which satisfies the expected opening-chapter pivot.
The 6 scene beats / 3 sequel beats ratio fits an action-forward opener and still gives breathing room after each major shift.
The closing beat withholds explanation but escalates stakes, so the chapter ends on forward pull rather than resolution.
character: Every beat stays legible through Sylvanas's limited-third filter; no beat requires omniscient access.
Sylvanas drives the chapter through repeated decisions: extending the patrol, taking the south road, and choosing immediate interrogation.
The only reactive stretch is the report landing in beat 4, and the chapter converts that reaction into agency by beat 5.
Lor'themar, Cyndia, the runner, and Jaina all have distinct narrative jobs, so no supporting character is redundant.
The arc-position promises are all delivered on-page: field ease, outsider pressure, forced choice, and the end-state shift into personal responsibility.
continuity: Chapter 1 has no prior-chapter memory to call back to, so all 9 beats are correctly annotated as new.
Character naming is internally consistent across outline and plan: Sylvanas, Lor'themar, Cyndia, and Jaina all match the established spellings.
The Jaina visual read in beat 8 is first-introduction material, not a reintroduction, so no anti-reintroduction correction is needed.
Chapter-close handoff facts align cleanly with the cast-and-handoff rules: Lor'themar northbound, Sylvanas/Jaina/Cyndia retained.
Callback density is 0%, which is appropriate for an opening chapter and avoids recap drag.
transitions: All 9 beats already contain transitionIntent coverage; no missing handoffs needed to be added.
Beat 3 to beat 4 is a clean hard interruption: easy trail rhythm breaks exactly where the runner arrives.
Beat 5 to beat 6 carries both logistical motion and emotional isolation after Lor'themar peels away.
Beat 6 to beat 7 lands the scene boundary coherently: the ride's arrival pressure resolves into Cyndia's factual briefing.
Beat 9 functions as a controlled chapter break rather than a dropped thread, because the interrogation hook explicitly hands momentum into chapter 2.
reshape: No beat reorder was necessary because the midpoint report already creates the chapter's structural turn.
No sequel beat had to be added because beats 2, 5, and 9 already absorb emotion and decision after each pressure spike.
No scene merge or split was required because the patrol/report/prisoner sequence tracks a clean escalation ladder.
No transition intents needed revision because each scene boundary already lands in the opening state of the next scene.
No continuity or anti-reintroduction repairs were needed beyond adding the chapter-start continuity annotations.

pacing.notes: The opener moves from active kill to banter to interruption without lingering too long in ease. Tension resets briefly in beats 2-3, pivots hard at beat 4, and climbs steadily through the Jaina reveal and interrogation hook.

structure.notes: Opening strength is high, the midpoint turn lands on the messenger report, and the close leaves a consequential unresolved question. Scene-sequel balance fits an action-led first chapter.

character.notes: Sylvanas owns the chapter's agency and filter. Supporting cast functions are distinct, and the beats fulfill the chapter's promised stance test and forced choice without premature thaw.

transitions.notes: All beat-level transitionIntent chains were verified. No missing intents. Scene boundaries from trail to report to holding space land cleanly. Chapter bridge is null because this is chapter 1.

continuity.notes: All 9 beats are new because there is no prior canonized chapter memory yet. No contradictions found. Callback density 0%. No anti-reintroductions required correction.
